steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the equation after $y = 3x$ is translated 6 units down. A vertical translation down by $k$ units subtracts $k$ from the output: $$y = 3x - 6$$

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = -6(3)^{x}$': changes the base to an exponential and introduces a negative coefficient — not a translation of $y = 3x$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y = 3^{(x+6)}$': rewrites $3x$ as an exponential $3^x$ and shifts the exponent — wrong function family entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 3x - 6$': subtracts 6 from the output of $y = 3x$, shifting the line 6 units down. ✓
  • Choice D — '$y = 3^{(x-6)}$': same exponential-rewrite trap as B, just with a rightward shift in the exponent. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
